pr.title: Add header and main struct for HSD_Shadow
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Added a new SysDolphin baselib header for HSD_Shadow, defining the main HSD_Shadow struct with object list, camera, texture, texture coordinate scale/translation fields, active state, intensity, and user data. The PR was a small type/header introduction with no discussion or review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Included global.h, dolphin/types.h, and baselib headers for cobj.h, tobj.h, and list.h.

Defined HSD_Shadow fields with documented offsets: objects at 0x0, camera at 0x4, texture at 0x8, scaleS at 0xC, scaleT at 0x10, transS at 0x14, transT at 0x18, active at 0x1C, intensity at 0x20, and user_data at 0x24.
Left a commented-out placeholder typedef for HSD_ShadowDesc.
smart_moves: Kept the change narrowly scoped to a single header and a single struct, making it easy to review and reuse.
Used existing baselib type names for related references, including HSD_SList, HSD_CObj, and HSD_TObj.
Annotated struct members with offsets, which is useful for decompilation alignment checks and future matching work.
Used forward-style struct pointer references, avoiding a need for full type definitions beyond the included baselib headers.
decomp_lessons: When introducing a new baselib type, include the minimum related headers needed to make referenced types discoverable.
Offset comments in struct definitions are valuable evidence for later verification of layout, padding, and field order.
A small header-only PR can unblock future source work by establishing canonical names and layout for a runtime object.
If a related descriptor type is known but not yet understood, a commented placeholder can signal intended future work, though it does not establish a usable type.
naming_conventions: Header file uses the lower-case subsystem object name: shadow.h.
Include guard follows the local style shown in the diff: _shadow_h_.
Runtime struct uses the HSD_ prefix and internal tag form struct _HSD_Shadow with typedef alias HSD_Shadow.
Field names use mixed conventions seen in the diff: camel-like texture coordinate fields scaleS, scaleT, transS, transT, and snake_case user_data.
Referenced baselib object types follow HSD_* naming: HSD_SList, HSD_CObj, HSD_TObj.
Boolean state field is named active and typed as BOOL.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserve exact field ordering and offsets for HSD_Shadow when matching code that accesses shadow object internals.
Use f32 for scaleS, scaleT, transS, and transT to match the documented 4-byte offsets.
Use BOOL for active at offset 0x1C, u8 for intensity at offset 0x20, and account for implied padding before user_data at offset 0x24.
The offset sequence implies the struct is at least 0x28 bytes on the target ABI, with pointer-sized fields at 4-byte boundaries.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that uncomment or define HSD_ShadowDesc.
Search for code using HSD_Shadow fields scaleS, scaleT, transS, transT, active, or intensity.
Check whether later style cleanups changed the include guard or field comments in src/sysdolphin/baselib/shadow.h.
Look for matching work in baselib shadow source files that relies on this struct layout.
